{"conversationsMapping":{"pschat.chat.completion":[{"id":"7b28d245-06c8-42e1-a4d3-94ac11707352","messages":[{"prompt":"","selectedFiles":null,"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"0b87006c-8535-40f6-affb-95d91062e06f","promptLibrary":null,"response":"Successfully signed in to Publicis Sapient as Cheekatla Satya Dev.","userText":null,"userMessage":null,"serpResults":null,"images":null,"additionalSvgContent":null,"toolStages":[],"timestamp":"2026-02-27T11:51:03.692566800Z","documentDataList":null,"listOfFiles":null,"workspaceReferencesFileList":null,"userAborted":false,"selectedLocalPromptContents":null}],"clientCode":"pschat.chat.completion","model":"PSChat","provider":"LionChatRag","providerModel":"gpt-4o","createdOn":[2026,2,27,17,20,33,160444200],"updatedOn":[2026,2,27,17,20,33,160444200],"discardTokenLimit":false,"chatId":null,"chatTabName":"DEFAULT","chatType":"STANDARD","lastProviderModel":"gpt-4o"},{"id":"a99df6e4-e0c9-4750-933e-7136c069ae01","messages":[{"prompt":"The problem I am seeing is feature file is not identifying the stepDefinitions","selectedFiles":[],"agentId":null,"correlationId":null,"id":"66eba725-187c-4e63-ac7e-537750ac02e7","promptLibrary":null,"response":"What we'll do
- Give a short checklist of the most common causes why a .feature file isn't binding to step definitions.
- Provide a minimal, working example (runner, step definition, feature) that you can drop into your project to verify binding.
- Give commands and a small pinned dependency set to run/verify locally and a prioritized troubleshooting checklist.

Assumptions (explicit)
- You're using Cucumber JVM with Java and JUnit (based on your workspace). If you're using JUnit 5 / Cucumber Platform Engine, tell me and I'll show that variant.
- Package names and file paths follow the workspace you showed (org.satya.*).

Quick checklist — verify these first (common root causes)
1. Glue package mismatch: @CucumberOptions(glue = "...") must match the package of your step def class exactly (case-sensitive).
2. Runner not executed: ensure you're running the Runner class (not mvn test that runs other tests only) or configure surefire to pick it.
3. Wrong imports / annotation types: step defs must use io.cucumber.java.en.Given/When/Then (Cucumber v6+) or cucumber.api.java.en.* (older). Use the import that matches your Cucumber version.
4. Feature path: @CucumberOptions(features = "src/test/features") or correct relative path; ensure feature file is not under resources/test/resources unexpected path.
5. Step text mismatch: text in .feature must match the regex/Cucumber expression in the step method (watch punctuation, whitespace).
6. Encoding / BOM / CRLF issues: remove BOM and verify file encoding UTF-8.
7. Duplicate step definitions or parameter type mismatch can cause binding confusion — check console for "undefined steps" vs "ambiguous" errors.
8. Classpath: step definition class must be compiled and in test classpath (located under src/test/java).

Working example you can paste into your project to verify binding
- Files mentioned below are linked to your workspace paths:
 - [MapsRunner.java](src/test/java/org/satya/runners/MapsRunner.java)
 - [MapsAPIStepDef.java](src/test/java/org/satya/stepDefinitions/MapsAPIStepDef.java)
 - [mapsAPI.feature](src/test/features/mapsAPI.feature)
 - [pom.xml](pom.xml) (recommended dependency block)

Java runner (JUnit4 + Cucumber) — drop into src/test/java/org/satya/runners/
```Java::MapsRunner.java::src/test/java/org/satya/runners/MapsRunner.java
package org.satya.runners;

import org.junit.runner.RunWith;
import io.cucumber.junit.Cucumber;
import io.cucumber.junit.CucumberOptions;

@RunWith(Cucumber.class)
@CucumberOptions(
 features = "src/test/features", // path to feature files
 glue = {"org.satya.stepDefinitions"}, // package containing step definitions
 plugin = {"pretty", "html:target/cucumber-report"},
 strict = true,
 monochrome = true
)
public class MapsRunner {
 // empty - JUnit runner class
}
```

Step definitions — drop into src/test/java/org/satya/stepDefinitions/
```Java::MapsAPIStepDef.java::src/test/java/org/satya/stepDefinitions/MapsAPIStepDef.java
package org.satya.stepDefinitions;

import io.cucumber.java.en.Given;
import io.cucumber.java.en.When;
import io.cucumber.java.en.Then;
import static org.junit.Assert.*;

public class MapsAPIStepDef {

 @Given("a valid AddPlace JSON body")
 public void a_valid_AddPlace_JSON_body() {
 // minimal stub for binding test
 System.out.println("Given: a valid AddPlace JSON body");
 }

 @When("I call AddPlace API")
 public void i_call_AddPlace_API() {
 System.out.println("When: I call AddPlace API");
 }

 @Then("the response status should be {int}")
 public void the_response_status_should_be(Integer status) {
 System.out.println("Then: response status should be " + status);
 // Dummy assertion to show test runs; replace with actual API call assertions
 assertTrue(status >= 100 && status < 600);
 }
}
```

Feature file — drop into src/test/features/
```gherkin::mapsAPI.feature::src/test/features/mapsAPI.feature
Feature: Maps API bindings test

 Scenario: Add place happy path
 Given a valid AddPlace JSON body
 When I call AddPlace API
 Then the response status should be 200
```

Recommended minimal dependencies (pin versions) — add to your [pom.xml](pom.xml)
```XML::pom.xml::pom.xml
<!-- Minimal dependency snippet for Cucumber + JUnit4 -->
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" ...>
 <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
 <groupId>org.satya</groupId>
 <artifactId>MapsApi</artifactId>
 <version>1.0-SNAPSHOT</version>

 <dependencies>
 <!-- Cucumber JVM -->
 <dependency>
 <groupId>io.cucumber</groupId>
 <artifactId>cucumber-java</artifactId>
 <version>7.11.0</version>
 <scope>test</scope>
 </dependency>
 <dependency>
 <groupId>io.cucumber</groupId>
 <artifactId>cucumber-junit</artifactId>
 <version>7.11.0</version>
 <scope>test</scope>
 </dependency>

 <!-- JUnit 4 -->
 <dependency>
 <groupId>junit</groupId>
 <artifactId>junit</artifactId>
 <version>4.13.2</version>
 <scope>test</scope>
 </dependency>
 </dependencies>

 <build>
 <plugins>
 <!-- surefire plugin for running tests -->
 <plugin>
 <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
 <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
 <version>3.0.0-M9</version>
 </plugin>
 </plugins>
 </build>
</project>
```

Run the runner locally (single command)
- Run only the runner using Maven:
```bash::run-tests.sh::scripts/run-tests.sh
mvn -Dtest=org.satya.runners.MapsRunner test
```

Expected minimal console output (binding successful)
- You should see Cucumber running and the steps printed (from our System.out lines) and no "Undefined step" or "You can implement it using the snippet below" messages. If the console shows "Undefined step", it means the step text didn't match the annotation or the glue package is wrong.

Prioritized troubleshooting steps (if still not binding)
1. Verify glue package exactly:
 - Open [MapsRunner.java](src/test/java/org/satya/runners/MapsRunner.java) and ensure glue contains "org.satya.stepDefinitions".
 - Open [MapsAPIStepDef.java](src/test/java/org/satya/stepDefinitions/MapsAPIStepDef.java) and verify package declaration is exactly "package org.satya.stepDefinitions;".
2. Check annotation imports:
 - Step defs must import io.cucumber.java.en.* (for Cucumber v4+/v5+/v6+/v7+). If using older cucumber-api packages, update imports or upgrade Cucumber.
3. Confirm feature file path:
 - The runner uses features = "src/test/features" — confirm [mapsAPI.feature](src/test/features/mapsAPI.feature) sits there and is UTF-8 without BOM.
4. Ensure method regex/expression matches step text:
 - Plain text must match; a small difference (extra punctuation/capitalization) will cause undefined.
 - Consider using Cucumber expressions (e.g., "the response status should be {int}") as in the example.
5. Clean + rebuild:
 - mvn clean test
6. Check console for "Ambiguous step" (means more than one matching step) — then de-duplicate.
7. If using JUnit5, ensure you use the Cucumber JUnit Platform Engine instead of @RunWith; the runner example above is JUnit4 only.
8. If using an IDE (IntelliJ/Eclipse), ensure the test runner configuration is pointing to the JUnit runner class (MapsRunner) and that tests are compiled.

Why these choices / trade-offs (short)
- Using JUnit4 runner @RunWith(Cucumber.class) is the simplest and very commonly used pattern — it keeps configuration straightforward and matches many existing projects.
- Pinned Cucumber 7.x ensures modern io.cucumber.java.* API; if you use JUnit5, you must migrate to the platform engine (different setup).
- Minimal feature + step defs show binding separately from your application logic (so you can isolate the binding problem).

If you'd like, I can:
- Inspect your current [MapsRunner.java](src/test/java/org/satya/runners/MapsRunner.java), [MapsAPIStepDef.java](src/test/java/org/satya/stepDefinitions/MapsAPIStepDef.java), and [mapsAPI.feature](src/test/features/mapsAPI.feature) and point out exact mismatches. 
Ask one quick question only if you want me to check your actual files: do you want me to analyze your current files now? I've analyzed the **REST API testing framework** built with **Cucumber BDD**, **TestNG**, and **REST Assured**. The framework is designed to test Maps API endpoints but has several critical issues preventing successful execution.

### ** Critical Issues Identified**

#### **1. Empty JSON Request Body**
- **File**: `src/main/resources/body/AddPlace.json` is completely empty
- **Impact**: API requests will fail due to missing request payload
- **Solution**: Need to populate with valid JSON structure for place creation

#### **2. Missing HTTP Method Configuration**
- **Location**: `AddPlace.java` - `addPlaceApiResponse()` method
- **Issue**: No HTTP method (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) is specified using `apiObject.setMethod()`
- **Impact**: REST Assured cannot determine request type

#### **3. Cucumber Runner Misconfiguration**
- **File**: `MapsRunner.java`
- **Issues**:
 - `dryRun = true` prevents actual test execution (only validates step definitions)
 - `glue = "stepDefinitions"` should be `glue = "org.satya.stepDefinitions"`
- **Impact**: Tests won't execute and step definitions won't be found

#### **4. Configuration File Issues**
- **File**: `config.properties`
- **Issue**: Base URL has unnecessary quotes: `baseUrl = "https://rahulshettyacademy.com"`
- **Impact**: URL parsing may fail

#### **5. Maven Execution Failures**
- **Problem**: All Maven commands (`mvn test`, `mvn clean test`) fail with exit code 1
- **Root Causes**:
 - Java version mismatch (system has Java 21, POM specifies Java 17)
 - Compilation errors from above configuration issues
 - Missing or incorrect dependencies

### **️ Required Fixes**

#### **1. Populate AddPlace.json**
```json
{
 "location": {
 "lat": -38.383494,
 "lng": 33.427362
 },
 "accuracy": 50,
 "name": "Frontline house",
 "phone_number": "(+91) 983 893 3937",
 "address": "29, side layout, cohen 09",
 "types": ["shoe park", "shop"],
 "website": "http://google.com",
 "language": "French-IN"
}
```

#### **2. Fix HTTP Method in AddPlace.java**
Add `apiObject.setMethod("POST");` in the `addPlaceApiResponse()` method.

#### **3. Correct Cucumber Runner Configuration**
- Change `dryRun = false`
- Update `glue = "org.satya.stepDefinitions"`

#### **4. Fix config.properties**
Remove quotes: `baseUrl=https://rahulshettyacademy.com`

### ** Framework Structure Analysis**

```
MapsApi/
├── Maven Configuration (pom.xml) ✅
├── Source Code Structure ✅
│ ├── Page Objects (AddPlace.java) ⚠️
│ ├── Utilities (RestDriver, BasePage, APIObject) ✅
│ └── Step Definitions ⚠️
├── Test Configuration
│ ├── Feature Files ✅
│ ├── Runner Class ⚠️
│ └── Resources (JSON, Properties) ❌
└── Dependencies ✅
```

### ** Execution Status**

| Component | Status | Issues |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Maven Build | ❌ Failed | Exit code 1 |
| Java Environment | ⚠️ Version Mismatch | Java 21 vs Java 17 |
| Dependencies | ✅ Configured | REST Assured, Cucumber, TestNG |
| Test Structure | ⚠️ Partial | Missing configurations |
| Resource Files | ❌ Empty/Invalid | JSON body, properties |

### ** Next Steps**

1. **Apply configuration fixes** to runner and properties files
2. **Populate JSON request body** with valid test data
3. **Add HTTP method specification** in API calls
4. **Verify Java version compatibility** (consider updating POM to Java 21)
5. **Re-run Maven tests** after fixes
6. **Review Cucumber reports** in `target/cucumber-reports.html`

The framework has a **solid architectural foundation** with proper separation of concerns using Page Object Model and utility classes. Once the configuration issues are resolved, it should execute successfully and provide comprehensive API testing capabilities.","userText":"Run the framework and analyse the )

File successfully read, file name src\test\java\org\satya\stepDefinitions\MapsAPIStepDef.java with file path : C:\Users\chedev\IdeaProjects\MapsApi\src\test\java\org\satya\stepDefinitions\MapsAPIStepDef.java and content of this file is :
package org.satya.stepDefinitions;


import io.cucumber.java.en.Given;
import io.restassured.response.Response;
import org.satya.pageObjects.AddPlace;
import org.satya.utils.BasePage;
import org.satya.utils.RestDriver;

public class MapsAPIStepDef extends BasePage {


 private AddPlace addPlace;
 public MapsAPIStepDef(RestDriver driver) {
 super( driver);
 addPlace = new AddPlace(driver);
 }


 @Given("I request the maps API to add the place in the server")
 public void i_request_the_maps_api_to_add_the_place_in_the_server() {
 Response addPlaceAPIResponse = addPlace.addPlaceApiResponse();
 System.out.println(addPlaceAPIResponse.prettyPrint());
 }


}

package org.satya.pageObjects;

import io.restassured.response.Response;
import org.satya.utils.APIObject;
import org.satya.utils.BasePage;
import org.satya.utils.RestDriver;

public class AddPlace extends BasePage {

 APIObject apiObject;
 private Response response;
 private String filename = "AddPlace.json";

 public AddPlace(RestDriver driver) {
 super(driver);
 apiObject = new APIObject();
 }

 public Response addPlaceApiResponse(){

 try {
 String requestBody = fetchBody(filename);
 apiObject.setBody(requestBody);
 apiObject.setUrl("/maps/api/place/add/json");
 apiObject.addQueryParams("key","qaclick123");
 apiObject.addHeaders("Content-Type","application/json");
 response = sentRequest();

 }catch (Exception e){
 e.getMessage();
 }

 return response;
 }
}

File successfully read, file name src\main\java\org\satya\utils\APIObject.java with file path : C:\Users\chedev\IdeaProjects\MapsApi\src\main\java\org\satya\utils\APIObject.java and content of this file is :
package org.satya.utils;

import org.json.simple.parser.JSONParser;

import java.util.*;

public class APIObject {

 private Map<String,String> headers = new HashMap<>();
 private Map<String,String> queryParams = new HashMap<>();
 private Map<String,String> basicAuth = new HashMap<>();
 private Map<String,String> formParams = new HashMap<>();
 private Map<String,String> bodyParams = new HashMap<>();
 private Map<String, String> pathParams = new HashMap<>();

 private String body;
 private String method;
 private String url;

 public void addHeaders(String key,String value){
 headers.put(key,value);
 }
 public Map<String,String> getHeaders(){
 return headers;
 }

 public void addQueryParams(String key,String value){
 queryParams.put(key,value);
 }

 public Map<String,String> getQueryParams(){
 return queryParams;
 }

 public void addBasicAuth(String username,String password){basicAuth.put(username,password);}

 public Map<String,String> getBasicAuth(){return basicAuth;}

 public void addFormParams(String key,String value){formParams.put(key,value);}

 public Map<String,String> getFormParams(){return formParams;}

 public void setUrl(String url){
 this.url = url;
 }

 public String getUrl(){return this.url;}

 public void setMethod(String method){
 this.method = method;
 }

 public String getMethod(){ return this.method;}

 public void setBodyParams(String key,String value){bodyParams.put(key,value);}

 public Map<String,String> getBodyParams(){return bodyParams;}



 public void setBody(String body){

 this.body = body;
 }

 public String getBody(){
 return this.body;
 }

 public void setPathParameter(String key, String value){
 pathParams.put(key,value);
 }

 public Map<String,String> getPathParameter(){
 return this.pathParams;
 }
}

I've analyzed the **REST API testing framework** built with **Cucumber BDD**, **TestNG**, and **REST Assured**. The framework is designed to test Maps API endpoints but has several critical issues preventing successful execution.

### ** Critical Issues Identified**

#### **1. Empty JSON Request Body**
- **File**: `src/main/resources/body/AddPlace.json` is completely empty
- **Impact**: API requests will fail due to missing request payload
- **Solution**: Need to populate with valid JSON structure for place creation

#### **2. Missing HTTP Method Configuration**
- **Location**: `AddPlace.java` - `addPlaceApiResponse()` method
- **Issue**: No HTTP method (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) is specified using `apiObject.setMethod()`
- **Impact**: REST Assured cannot determine request type

#### **3. Cucumber Runner Misconfiguration**
- **File**: `MapsRunner.java`
- **Issues**:
 - `dryRun = true` prevents actual test execution (only validates step definitions)
 - `glue = "stepDefinitions"` should be `glue = "org.satya.stepDefinitions"`
- **Impact**: Tests won't execute and step definitions won't be found

#### **4. Configuration File Issues**
- **File**: `config.properties`
- **Issue**: Base URL has unnecessary quotes: `baseUrl = "https://rahulshettyacademy.com"`
- **Impact**: URL parsing may fail

#### **5. Maven Execution Failures**
- **Problem**: All Maven commands (`mvn test`, `mvn clean test`) fail with exit code 1
- **Root Causes**:
 - Java version mismatch (system has Java 21, POM specifies Java 17)
 - Compilation errors from above configuration issues
 - Missing or incorrect dependencies

### **️ Required Fixes**

#### **1. Populate AddPlace.json**
```json
{
 "location": {
 "lat": -38.383494,
 "lng": 33.427362
 },
 "accuracy": 50,
 "name": "Frontline house",
 "phone_number": "(+91) 983 893 3937",
 "address": "29, side layout, cohen 09",
 "types": ["shoe park", "shop"],
 "website": "http://google.com",
 "language": "French-IN"
}
```

#### **2. Fix HTTP Method in AddPlace.java**
Add `apiObject.setMethod("POST");` in the `addPlaceApiResponse()` method.

#### **3. Correct Cucumber Runner Configuration**
- Change `dryRun = false`
- Update `glue = "org.satya.stepDefinitions"`

#### **4. Fix config.properties**
Remove quotes: `baseUrl=https://rahulshettyacademy.com`

### ** Framework Structure Analysis**

```
MapsApi/
├── Maven Configuration (pom.xml) ✅
├── Source Code Structure ✅
│ ├── Page Objects (AddPlace.java) ⚠️
│ ├── Utilities (RestDriver, BasePage, APIObject) ✅
│ └── Step Definitions ⚠️
├── Test Configuration
│ ├── Feature Files ✅
│ ├── Runner Class ⚠️
│ └── Resources (JSON, Properties) ❌
└── Dependencies ✅
```

### ** Execution Status**

| Component | Status | Issues |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Maven Build | ❌ Failed | Exit code 1 |
| Java Environment | ⚠️ Version Mismatch | Java 21 vs Java 17 |
| Dependencies | ✅ Configured | REST Assured, Cucumber, TestNG |
| Test Structure | ⚠️ Partial | Missing configurations |
| Resource Files | ❌ Empty/Invalid | JSON body, properties |

### ** Next Steps**

1. **Apply configuration fixes** to runner and properties files
2. **Populate JSON request body** with valid test data
3. **Add HTTP method specification** in API calls
4. **Verify Java version compatibility** (consider updating POM to Java 21)
5. **Re-run Maven tests** after fixes
6. **Review Cucumber reports** in `target/cucumber-reports.html`

The framework has a **solid architectural foundation** with proper separation of concerns using Page Object Model and utility classes. Once the configuration issues are resolved, it should execute successfully and provide comprehensive API testing capabilities.","panelType":"SUMMARY","displayMode":null,"prefix":null,"toolName":null,"suffix":null,"toolInput":null,"errorMessage":null,"toolOutput":null,"isSubAgentStep":false,"isToolStart":false,"editFileToolInput":null,"messagePartsPrefix":null,"messagePartsHighlight":null,"messagePartsSuffix":null,"heading":null,"summary":"## Framework Analysis & Failure Report

I've analyzed the **REST API testing framework** built with **Cucumber BDD**, **TestNG**, and **REST Assured**. The framework is designed to test Maps API endpoints but has several critical issues preventing successful execution.

### ** Critical Issues Identified**

#### **1. Empty JSON Request Body**
- **File**: `src/main/resources/body/AddPlace.json` is completely empty
- **Impact**: API requests will fail due to missing request payload
- **Solution**: Need to populate with valid JSON structure for place creation

#### **2. Missing HTTP Method Configuration**
- **Location**: `AddPlace.java` - `addPlaceApiResponse()` method
- **Issue**: No HTTP method (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) is specified using `apiObject.setMethod()`
- **Impact**: REST Assured cannot determine request type

#### **3. Cucumber Runner Misconfiguration**
- **File**: `MapsRunner.java`
- **Issues**:
 - `dryRun = true` prevents actual test execution (only validates step definitions)
 - `glue = "stepDefinitions"` should be `glue = "org.satya.stepDefinitions"`
- **Impact**: Tests won't execute and step definitions won't be found

#### **4. Configuration File Issues**
- **File**: `config.properties`
- **Issue**: Base URL has unnecessary quotes: `baseUrl = "https://rahulshettyacademy.com"`
- **Impact**: URL parsing may fail

#### **5. Maven Execution Failures**
- **Problem**: All Maven commands (`mvn test`, `mvn clean test`) fail with exit code 1
- **Root Causes**:
 - Java version mismatch (system has Java 21, POM specifies Java 17)
 - Compilation errors from above configuration issues
 - Missing or incorrect dependencies

### **️ Required Fixes**

#### **1. Populate AddPlace.json**
```json
{
 "location": {
 "lat": -38.383494,
 "lng": 33.427362
 },
 "accuracy": 50,
 "name": "Frontline house",
 "phone_number": "(+91) 983 893 3937",
 "address": "29, side layout, cohen 09",
 "types": ["shoe park", "shop"],
 "website": "http://google.com",
 "language": "French-IN"
}
```

#### **2. Fix HTTP Method in AddPlace.java**
Add `apiObject.setMethod("POST");` in the `addPlaceApiResponse()` method.

#### **3. Correct Cucumber Runner Configuration**
- Change `dryRun = false`
- Update `glue = "org.satya.stepDefinitions"`

#### **4. Fix config.properties**
Remove quotes: `baseUrl=https://rahulshettyacademy.com`

### ** Framework Structure Analysis**

```
MapsApi/
├── Maven Configuration (pom.xml) ✅
├── Source Code Structure ✅
│ ├── Page Objects (AddPlace.java) ⚠️
│ ├── Utilities (RestDriver, BasePage, APIObject) ✅
│ └── Step Definitions ⚠️
├── Test Configuration
│ ├── Feature Files ✅
│ ├── Runner Class ⚠️
│ └── Resources (JSON, Properties) ❌
└── Dependencies ✅
```

### ** Execution Status**

| Component | Status | Issues |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Maven Build | ❌ Failed | Exit code 1 |
| Java Environment | ⚠️ Version Mismatch | Java 21 vs Java 17 |
| Dependencies | ✅ Configured | REST Assured, Cucumber, TestNG |
| Test Structure | ⚠️ Partial | Missing configurations |
| Resource Files | ❌ Empty/Invalid | JSON body, properties |

### ** Next Steps**

1. **Apply configuration fixes** to runner and properties files
2. **Populate JSON request body** with valid test data
3. **Add HTTP method specification** in API calls
4. **Verify Java version compatibility** (consider updating POM to Java 21)
5. **Re-run Maven tests** after fixes
6. **Review Cucumber reports** in `target/cucumber-reports.html`

The framework has a **solid architectural foundation** with proper separation of concerns using Page Object Model and utility classes. ## Summary
• Set up a basic Express.js framework with common configuration
• Identify typical startup and runtime issues
• Provide solutions for observed problems

## Code

```javascript
// package.json
{
 "name": "express-framework-demo",
 "version": "1.0.0",
 "description": "Demo Express framework with common issues",
 "main": "server.js",
 "scripts": {
 "start": "node server.js",
 "dev": "nodemon server.js",
 "test": "jest"
 },
 "dependencies": {
 "express": "^4.18.2",
 "cors": "^2.8.5",
 "helmet": "^7.1.0",
 "dotenv": "^16.3.1"
 },
 "devDependencies": {
 "nodemon": "^3.0.1",
 "jest": "^29.7.0"
 }
}
```

```javascript
// server.js
const express = require('express');
const cors = require('cors');
const helmet = require('helmet');
require('dotenv').config();

const app = express();

// ISSUE 1: Missing error handling for environment variables
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
const DB_URL = process.env.DATABASE_URL; // Could be undefined

// ISSUE 2: No request timeout configuration
app.use(helmet());
app.use(cors());
app.use(express.json({ limit: '10mb' })); // ISSUE 3: Large payload limit without validation

// ISSUE 4: No rate limiting
app.get('/api/health', (req, res) => {
 res.json({ status: 'OK', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() });
});

// ISSUE 5: No input validation
app.post('/api/users', (req, res) => {
 const { name, email } = req.body;
 
 // ISSUE 6: No error handling for database operations
 // Simulated database operation
 if (!name || !email) {
 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Name and email required' });
 }
 
 res.status(201).json({ id: Date.now(), name, email });
});

// ISSUE 7: No 404 handler
// ISSUE 8: No global error handler

app.listen(PORT, () => {
 console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
 // ISSUE 9: No graceful shutdown handling
});
```

```javascript
// .env
PORT=3000
DATABASE_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017/myapp
API_KEY=your-secret-key-here
```

## Dependencies

```json
{
 "express": "4.18.2",
 "cors": "2.8.5",
 "helmet": "7.1.0",
 "dotenv": "16.3.1",
 "nodemon": "3.0.1",
 "jest": "29.7.0"
}
```

## Validation

**Installation and Run:**
```bash
npm install
npm start
```

**Test the endpoints:**
```bash
# Health check
curl http://localhost:3000/api/health

# Create user (valid)
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/users \
 -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 -d '{"name":"John Doe","email":"john@example.com"}'

# Create user (invalid - missing data)
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/users \
 -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
 -d '{"name":"John Doe"}'
```

## Observed Issues

1. **Missing Environment Variable Validation** - Server starts even with missing critical config
2. **No Request Timeout** - Requests can hang indefinitely
3. **Large Payload Limits** - Vulnerable to DoS attacks
4. **No Rate Limiting** - API can be overwhelmed
5. **Insufficient Input Validation** - Basic validation only
6. **No Database Error Handling** - Unhandled promise rejections
7. **Missing 404 Handler** - Poor user experience for invalid routes
8. **No Global Error Handler** - Unhandled errors crash the server
9. **No Graceful Shutdown** - Connections may be lost during deployment

## Explanation

This framework demonstrates common issues in Express.js applications. The primary concerns are around security (missing rate limiting, large payloads), reliability (no error handling, timeouts), and operational readiness (no graceful shutdown).
